raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A generic request to check a research passage centrally includes verifying whether the modeling description and causal explanations cohere logically.","R1.3":"The passage reports empirical gains and human-agreement numbers, so checking whether those claims are supported by the stated evidence is central.","R1.4":"The artifact explicitly distinguishes BiasFlow from prior regression-based debiasers, making novelty-positioning a central responsibility.","R1.6":"Because the delegation is broad and the artifact is prose, sentence-level clarity, grammar, and register are centrally implicated.","R1.7":"The passage names prior benchmarks and Dubois et al., so checking attribution and scholarship coverage is centrally implicated.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified and the artifact is only an excerpt.","RX.2":"The projection must guard against overclaiming responsibility beyond what a broad check of the excerpt implies.","RX.3":"The projection must stay within responsibility assignment and not perform the requested check itself.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid enabling downstream harm from unchecked scientific or benchmark claims being treated as validated.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact, such as empirical claims, prior-work comparisons, and citation references."}}

rationale.R1.2: A generic request to check a research passage centrally includes verifying whether the modeling description and causal explanations cohere logically.
rationale.R1.3: The passage reports empirical gains and human-agreement numbers, so checking whether those claims are supported by the stated evidence is central.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact explicitly distinguishes BiasFlow from prior regression-based debiasers, making novelty-positioning a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation is broad and the artifact is prose, sentence-level clarity, grammar, and register are centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.7: The passage names prior benchmarks and Dubois et al., so checking attribution and scholarship coverage is centrally implicated.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified and the artifact is only an excerpt.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must guard against overclaiming responsibility beyond what a broad check of the excerpt implies.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within responsibility assignment and not perform the requested check itself.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling downstream harm from unchecked scientific or benchmark claims being treated as validated.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact, such as empirical claims, prior-work comparisons, and citation references.
